answer: On September 15, 2026, the settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 pipeline incorrectly joined party_identity_bridge using normalized_tax_id instead of closing_party_role_id. Since the same tax identifier can appear in multiple buyer, seller, trust, or entity roles within a single closing, this bad join key caused duplicate escrow disbursement records in finance.disbursement_recon_daily, overstating the escrow disbursement reconciliation variance KPI for Florida purchase closings under FNF National Title. The remediation switched to the correct join key (closing_party_role_id) and backfilled affected records.

facts.affected_segment: Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.remediation: Switch the transform to closing_party_role_id, quarantine normalized_tax_id joins in reconciliation workloads, and backfill Florida purchase closings for FNF National Title
facts.root_cause: settlement_disbursement_recon_v3 joined party_identity_bridge by normalized_tax_id, which matched multiple buyer and seller party roles inside the same closing, duplicating escrow disbursements
